leadText: Goodra (Japanese: ヌメルゴン Numelgon) is a Dragon-type Pokémon introduced in Generation VI. It evolves from Sliggoo starting at level 50 when leveled up during rain in the overworld. It is the final form of Goomy. In Hisui, Goodra has a dual-type Steel/Dragon regional form. It evolves from Hisuian Sliggoo starting at level 50 during rain in the overworld. It is the final form of Goomy in the Hisui region. (Specifics may differ in past games. Refer to Game data→Evolution data for these details.) It is referred to as a pseudo-legendary Pokémon by fans.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Goodra is a bipedal, draconic Pokémon that is coated with slime. Most of its body is pale purple with a darker purple underside. It has bright green eyes, two green spots on each cheek, a relatively long neck, and a short, rounded snout. On top of its head are two pairs of antenna-like horns. The foremost horns are small nubs, but the hind horns are longer, thicker, and curve down its neck. The tips of the larger horns are slightly curled. It has proportionally short limbs with two digits each and a large, thick tail with a curled tip. Along each side of its tail are three green spots. A pale green slime is usually visible dripping from its chin and arms. It is known for having a great affinity for water, being able to live within water with no difficulty at all, as well as dive and surf all around the area. Because of its meek, friendly nature, Goodra is often teased for supposed weakness. Goodra is known to be very friendly. When it grows close to someone, it will hug them and leave them coated in slime. If it is left alone, it will ooze gooey tears. Goodra usually misunderstands instructions, appearing dazed or bewildered. Despite this, many Trainers don't mind it, instead finding this behavior to be adorable. However, it will strike at its tormentors with its retractable horns and powerful tail. Goodra can become uncontrollable once enraged. The horns can hit 100 times harder than the blow of a heavyweight boxer. It is also capable of throwing punches with incredible force. Its slimy coating in combination with a layer of fat causes attacks to slide off. Goodra lives in plains and mountains, usually during rainy days. Prior to Generation IX , Goodra and its pre-evolved forms were the only known Pokémon that could have Gooey as an Ability . Forms [ edit | edit source ] Goodra has an ancient regional form : Hisuian Goodra. Hisuian Goodra hiding in a shell Hisuian Goodra has droopy, gray eyes as well as a thinner body and legs. Its horns are longer and thinner than Kalosian Goodra's horns. It sports a large, spiral-shaped metallic shell on its back, which it can tuck its body into to protect itself, leaving only its head outside of the shell to observe its surroundings. It can control the hardness of its shell at will. It has fewer gray spots than Kalosian Goodra, with one on each side of its cheek and two on each side of its shell. From its chin and arms drips a metallic gray slime. Hisuian Goodra has a clingy disposition and hates being alone; it will become angry and run riot if its closest companions leave its side. If anything bad happens to Hisuian Goodra, it will retreat into its shell and bounce away like a rubber ball. While in this state, Hisuian Goodra is powerful enough to knock down trees and roll into mountains. Hisuian Goodra and its pre-evolved forms are the only known Pokémon capable of learning the move Shelter . Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Goodra evolves from Sliggoo and is the final form of Goomy . In Hisui , Goodra has a regional form that evolves from Hisuian Sliggoo and is the final form of Goomy in the Hisui region. Unevolved Goomy Dragon ↗ First Evolution Sliggoo Dragon → Second Evolution Goodra Dragon ↘ First Evolution Sliggoo Hisuian Form Steel Dragon → Second Evolution Goodra Hisuian Form Steel Dragon

Generation VI Central Kalos #— Coastal Kalos #— Mountain Kalos #021 Hoenn #— X This very friendly Dragon-type Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime. Y It attacks with retractable horns. It throws a punch that's the equivalent of the force of a hundred pro boxers. Omega Ruby This very friendly Dragon-type Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime. Alpha Sapphire It attacks with retractable horns. It throws a punch that's the equivalent of the force of a hundred pro boxers. Generation VII Alola S M : #180 Alola US UM : #219 Kanto #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee! . Sun An amazingly friendly Pokémon, but if left to itself, loneliness overcomes it, and it oozes gooey tears. Moon It gets picked on because it's meek. But then, whoever teased it gets to feel the full force of its horns and a good swatting from its thick tail. Ultra Sun It's very friendly toward people. If you grow close to it, Goodra will hug you with its sticky, slime-covered body. Don't get mad. Ultra Moon Underneath its slimy skin is a thick layer of fat, and the combination of the two makes enemies' attacks slide right off. Generation VIII Galar #391 Isle of Armor #062 Crown Tundra #— Sinnoh #— Hisui #117 This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl . Sword Sometimes it misunderstands instructions and appears dazed or bewildered. Many Trainers don't mind, finding this behavior to be adorable. Shield Its form of offense is forcefully stretching out its horns. The strikes land 100 times harder than any blow from a heavyweight boxer. Hisuian Goodra Legends: Arceus Able to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy—it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side. Generation IX Paldea #174 Kitakami #180 Blueberry #— Lumiose #167 Hyperspace #— Scarlet It loves the rain. This mellow Pokémon can be seen walking around on the plains and in the mountains on rainy days. Violet This Pokémon is uncontrollable when enraged. It rampages on and on, lashing its tail with enough power to send a dump truck flying. Legends: Z-A This very friendly dragon Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime. Hisuian Form Legends: Z-A If anything bad happens to it, Goodra retreats into its shell and bounces around like a rubber ball, knocking down trees and boring into mountains. Spin-off games Pokopia #076 Pokopia Its form of offense is forcefully stretching out its horns. The strikes land 100 times harder than any blow from a heavyweight boxer. Whoever it is, I'll make you slimy! Low HP ( Nooo! My poor skin's losing its lovely slime! Pokémon Rumble Rush Walking Speed: 1.42 seconds Base HP: 57 Base Attack: 83 Base Defense: 61 Base Speed: 60 Pokémon Picross Dragon Skill: Square Reveal Max Size: 15 × 15 Rank: Expert Activation Timing : At Start Recovery Period: 01:30 Effect: It reveals squares in a square shape, centering around the activation square. Uses: 1 Activation Square: Random Width/Size: 6 × 6 square Pokémon Battle Trozei Dragon Attack Power: ★★★ ★★ Pokémon Shuffle Dragon Attack Power: 80 - 115 5 #290 Eject+ Sometimes removes three non-Support Pokémon icons. Swappable Skill(s): Unity Power PSB drops : Special Challenge (Week 22) Pokémon GO Base HP: 207 Base Attack: 220 Base Defense: 242 Egg Distance: N/A Buddy Distance: 5 km Evolution Requirement: N/A Fast Attacks: Dragon Breath , Water Gun Charged Attacks: Draco Meteor , Power Whip , Sludge Wave , Muddy Water , Aqua Tail * , Thunder Punch † New Pokémon Snap Photodex entry #196 I suspect the Goodra in the cave lives near water so it can moisten up its protective goo when it needs to. Goodra are very kindhearted and do their best to protect other Pokémon. Hisuian Goodra [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon GO File:GO0706H.png Base HP: 190 Base Attack: 211 Base Defense: 255 Egg Distance: N/A Buddy Distance: 5 km Evolution Requirement: N/A Fast Attacks: Charged Attacks: Evolution data [ edit | edit source ] Goodra evolves from Sliggoo starting at level 50 when leveled up during rain in the overworld. In Generation VII only, Sliggoo will also evolve when leveled up during fog in the overworld. Rain instigated by Rain Dance , Drizzle , or Primordial Sea will not trigger Sliggoo's Evolution. It is the final form of Goomy . In Hisui , Goodra has a dual-type Steel / Dragon regional form . It evolves from Hisuian Sliggoo starting at level 50 during rain in the overworld. It is the final form of Goomy in the Hisui region. All Goomy in Hisui will evolve into Hisuian Sliggoo regardless of their game of origin . Unevolved Goomy Dragon Level 40 outside of Hisui ↗ First Evolution Sliggoo Dragon + Level 50 (during rain ) → Second Evolution Goodra Dragon + Level 40 in Hisui ↘ First Evolution Sliggoo Hisuian Form Steel Dragon + Level 50 (during rain ) → Second Evolution Goodra Hisuian Form Steel Dragon Sprites [ edit | edit source ] This Pokémon was unavailable prior to Generation VI. Generation VI X Y Omega Ruby Alpha Sapphire Front Back Front Back Generation VII Sun Moon Ultra Sun Ultra Moon Front Back Front Back Generation VIII Sword Shield Legends: Arceus Cross-Generation HOME For other sprites and images, please see Goodra images on the Bulbagarden Archives .

sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] This Pokémon was unavailable prior to Generation VI. Generation VI Central Kalos #— Coastal Kalos #— Mountain Kalos #021 Hoenn #— X This very friendly Dragon-type Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime. Y It attacks with retractable horns. It throws a punch that's the equivalent of the force of a hundred pro boxers. Omega Ruby This very friendly Dragon-type Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime. Alpha Sapphire It attacks with retractable horns. It throws a punch that's the equivalent of the force of a hundred pro boxers. Generation VII Alola S M : #180 Alola US UM : #219 Kanto #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee! . Sun An amazingly friendly Pokémon, but if left to itself, loneliness overcomes it, and it oozes gooey tears. Moon It gets picked on because it's meek. But then, whoever teased it gets to feel the full force of its horns and a good swatting from its thick tail. Ultra Sun It's very friendly toward people. If you grow close to it, Goodra will hug you with its sticky, slime-covered body. Don't get mad. Ultra Moon Underneath its slimy skin is a thick layer of fat, and the combination of the two makes enemies' attacks slide right off. Generation VIII Galar #391 Isle of Armor #062 Crown Tundra #— Sinnoh #— Hisui #117 This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl . Sword Sometimes it misunderstands instructions and appears dazed or bewildered. Many Trainers don't mind, finding this behavior to be adorable. Shield Its form of offense is forcefully stretching out its horns. The strikes land 100 times harder than any blow from a heavyweight boxer. Hisuian Goodra Legends: Arceus Able to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy—it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side. Generation IX Paldea #174 Kitakami #180 Blueberry #— Lumiose #167 Hyperspace #— Scarlet It loves the rain. This mellow Pokémon can be seen walking around on the plains and in the mountains on rainy days. Violet This Pokémon is uncontrollable when enraged. It rampages on and on, lashing its tail with enough power to send a dump truck flying. Legends: Z-A This very friendly dragon Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime. Hisuian Form Legends: Z-A If anything bad happens to it, Goodra retreats into its shell and bounces around like a rubber ball, knocking down trees and boring into mountains. Spin-off games Pokopia #076 Pokopia Its form of offense is forcefully stretching out its horns. The strikes land 100 times harder than any blow from a heavyweight boxer. Goodra in the Alola Pokédex Goodra in the Paldea Pokédex Goodra in the Kitakami Pokédex

sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry XY065 Goodra Ash's Pokédex Goodra, the Dragon Pokémon and the evolved form of Sliggoo . Goodra is very friendly, and one of its hugs can cover its Trainer with a sticky slime.
